assessment_items.question_content:
**Luther taught that Christians should follow . . .**

[[☃ radio 1]]
hints:
Luther believed that Christians should follow the Bible and not the teachings of the Catholic Church, which led to the translation of the Bible into vernacular languages.
[Watch this video to review](/humanities/renaissance-reformation/protestant-reformation1/v/introduction-to-the-protestant-reformation-luther-2-of-4)

choices.content: the Bible.
the teachings of the Catholic Church.
the teachings of all Protestant denominations.
Henry VIII.

assessment_items.question_content:
**Before the Protestant Reformation, the Catholic Church was . . .**

[[☃ radio 1]]
hints:
Before the Protestant Reformation, the Catholic Church was the only church in Western Europe.
[Read this article to review](/humanities/renaissance-reformation/protestant-reformation1/a/the-protestant-reformation)

choices.content: the only church in Western Europe.
one of only a few different kinds of churches in Western Europe.
one of many different kinds of churches in Western Europe.
pushing out other churches in Western Europe.

assessment_items.question_content:
**Pope Leo X wanted to rebuild Old St. Peter's Basilica with an ambitious new building. Leo financed the new building in part through . . . **

[[☃ radio 1]]
hints:
The new building was expensive and the Catholic Church decided to sell indulgences, which gave people time off from Purgatory.

[Watch this video to review](/humanities/renaissance-reformation/protestant-reformation1/v/protestant-reformation-1)

choices.content: the sale of indulgences.
loans from the Byzantine Empire.
plunder from the New World.
the sale of territories in the New World to Spain

assessment_items.question_content:
**Zwingli taught that during the Eucharist (Communion) . . .**

[[☃ radio 1]]
hints:
Zwingli taught that the Eucharist (Communion) was purely symbolic and that the flesh and blood of Christ was not present in the bread and wine, as Luther taught.

[Watch this video for review](/humanities/renaissance-reformation/protestant-reformation1/v/introduction-to-the-protestant-reformation-3-of-4)

choices.content: the bread and the wine were purely symbolic.
the bread and the wine transformed into the body and blood of Christ.
that Christ is present in the bread and the wine, and no transformation occurs.
the bread and wine are not necessary for Christian worship.

hints:
The Catholic Church reaffirmed its doctrines at the Council of Trent, specifically regarding the role of good works and church teaching in issues relating to salvation, and the Church also reaffirmed the miracle of transubstantiation. The priesthood of all believers and adult baptism were Protestant - not Catholic - beliefs.

[Watch this video to review](/humanities/renaissance-reformation/protestant-reformation1/v/introduction-to-the-protestant-reformation-the-counter-reformation-4-of-4)

choices.content: reaffirmed the doctrines of the Catholic Church.
reaffirmed the role of good works and church teaching on salvation.
reaffirmed the miracle of transubstantiation.
reaffirmed the priesthood of all believers.
reaffirmed the necessity of adult baptism.

assessment_items.question_content:
**Which of the following statements are true of the Counter-Reformation?**

[[☃ radio 1]]
hints:
The Council of Trent affirmed the role of art in the Catholic Church. During the Counter-Reformation, the Catholic Church made a great effort to spread Catholicism, especially to newly discovered places. As part of the Counter-Reformation, the Catholic Church tried to stamp out heresy.

[Watch this video to review](/humanities/renaissance-reformation/protestant-reformation1/v/introduction-to-the-protestant-reformation-the-counter-reformation-4-of-4)

choices.content: The Council of Trent affirmed the role of art in the Catholic Church.
During the Counter-Reformation, the Catholic Church made a great effort to spread Catholicism, especially to newly discovered places.
As part of the Counter-Reformation, the Catholic Church tried to stamp out heresy.
The Council of Trent affirmed that faith was the only path to salvation.
During the Counter-Reformation, the Catholic Church tolerated differences of opinion on church doctrine.
As part of the Counter-Reformation, the Catholic Church tried to get rid of religious images.

hints:
The Protestant Reformation brought about the creation of many new churches, where before there had only been the Catholic Church in Western Europe.

[Watch this video to review](/humanities/renaissance-reformation/protestant-reformation1/v/protestant-reformation-1)

choices.content: the creation of many new Protestant churches.
a single, unified Protestant Church.
the unification of the Protestant and Catholic Churches.
the Catholic Church.

hints:
Luther believed in the priesthood of all believers, meaning an individual and unmediated relationship with God.
[Watch this video to review](/humanities/renaissance-reformation/protestant-reformation1/v/introduction-to-the-protestant-reformation-3-of-4)

choices.content: in the priesthood of all believers.
in the priesthood of the popes, who descended from St. Peter.
in a new Protestant priesthood.
in the ordination of women.
